tobe_workflows.name: Lead Capture and Attribution
tobe_workflows.copy: As-is triggers; fix gclid/fbclid capture. Copies attribution (utm_source, utm_campaign, gclid, fbclid, lead_source) onto the Opportunity at create, so wins and losses both carry it. Speed-to-lead rule: the welcome email goes out instantly; a lead that arrives outside quiet hours (for example 11 PM) has its speed-to-lead SMS queued to 8 AM Contact Timezone (TCPA-safe). Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone on all SMS sends.

tobe_workflows.name: Non-Booked Recovery
tobe_workflows.copy: 10-touch non-booked recovery (5 SMS, 4 email, 1 voicemail over 16 days). Exits immediately if the lead books (to WF-03); after 16 days hands off to WF-09. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Booking Confirmation and Reminders
tobe_workflows.copy: Absorbs booking confirmation and all reminder sends. R1 reminder cadence (T-3d / T-1d / morning-of / T-2h) plus a telehealth branch. A cancellation halts reminders and routes to WF-08. Appointment-relative waits use an 'if date already passed' fallback. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Medical Intake Chase
tobe_workflows.copy: WF-04 owns all intake chasing going forward (standalone 'Intake Reminder' workflow is retired at cutover, Decision 9). R1 intake nudge cadence. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Clinic Outcome Router
tobe_workflows.copy: P0: remove auto-Won (Decision 10). The PCC Sales Form is the sole writer of sale_outcome, sale_type, and value; WF-05 is a router that reacts to the form-written values (never writes outcomes). First gates on appt_status: showed routes to outcome handling; no-show or cancel routes to WF-08; reschedule routes back to WF-03. Idempotency: WF-05 checks opportunity.outcome_processed_at before running and stamps it after, so an edit or a duplicate submit updates fields but never re-fires routing or the ad conversion. On Sold it sets renewal_date (Won date + term) and branches on sale_type: New to new-patient onboarding, Renewal to the Retention and Renewals lifecycle. MAR keeps the Opportunity Open and opens a PCC/provider task. A 'no form by end of appointment day' chaser nags the PCC only and writes no outcome.

tobe_workflows.name: Post-Visit Won and Onboarding
tobe_workflows.copy: Post-visit onboarding sequence: welcome, week-1 progress, NPS survey at 14 days, conditional Google review request, and a negative-NPS internal alert. NEW-patient path; renewals route to the Retention and Renewals lifecycle instead. Survey submission writes opportunity.visit_feedback_score, which drives the NPS branch. WF-05 owns renewal_date; WF-06 no longer sets it. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Post-Visit No-Sale Nurture
tobe_workflows.copy: 11-touch no-sale (nosale) objection-handling sequence over 35 days, targeting the declared nosale_reason. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: No-Show and Cancel Recovery
tobe_workflows.copy: 8-touch no-show and cancel recovery over 7 days. Exits immediately at any point if the member re-books (to WF-03); after 7 days with no re-engagement hands off to WF-09. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Long-Term Nurture
tobe_workflows.copy: Long-Term Nurture main sequence (7 touches over 120 days) plus an explicitly labeled Renewal Reminders sub-flow (30 / 21 / 14 / 7 / day-of before opportunity.renewal_date) for won members. Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone.

tobe_workflows.name: Feedback Survey
tobe_workflows.copy: Feedback survey. Keep as-is bodies where good; survey submission writes opportunity.visit_feedback_score for the WF-06 NPS branch. All sends filter on sms_consent_status + native DND and run inside quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone (WF-11 gate).

tobe_workflows.name: Compliance and Errors
tobe_workflows.copy: Single DND and consent authority. Filters every send on canonical sms_consent_status + native DND. Assume-consent for the 11,077 total historical contacts is an accepted risk (Decision 8).

tobe_workflows.name: Call Disposition Handler
tobe_workflows.copy: Handles call dispositions from softphone and disposition workflows; writes opportunity.call_disposition and reads patient_care_consultant_id (the PCC Sales Form is the sole writer of that field). Routes negative-NPS follow-ups. See the dedicated WF-12 diagram.

tobe_workflows.name: Ad-Platform Conversions
tobe_workflows.copy: Booked and Won only; exclude no-sale (nosale) outcomes.

tobe_workflows.name: Ambassador Program
tobe_workflows.copy: Post-sale ambassador activation: invitation, program welcome, and reward confirmation. Referred contacts are always created as NEW contacts and routed through WF-15. All sends filter on sms_consent_status + native DND and run inside quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone (WF-11 gate).

tobe_workflows.name: PCC Referral Routing
tobe_workflows.copy: Routes PCC-submitted referrals into the Referrals pipeline (Submitted stage) with a referred-lead welcome (SMS + email) and an internal routing alert. The Referrals pipeline is tracking-only (no monetary value); the referred contact is created NEW and routed back to WF-01, and its Sales opportunity carries all revenue. All sends filter on sms_consent_status + native DND and run inside quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone (WF-11 gate).

tobe_workflows.name: Comms Edge
tobe_workflows.absorbs: 1. IVR - simultaneous, Chat OOO, Missed Call Text-Back
tobe_workflows.copy: Missed-call text-back plus an explicit simultaneous-ring IVR path (absorbs '1. IVR - simultaneous'). Quiet hours 8 AM to 9 PM Contact Timezone on outbound sends.

defects.title: Auto-Won on attendance, not purchase
defects.evidence: WF 05 step name: 'A&D pipeline: Closed, status: WON - hide from a&D opportunity'
defects.impact: 939 false wins (~50% of all wins account-wide); revenue overstated

defects.title: All revenue/outcome on Contact, not Opportunity
defects.impact: Outcomes overwrite per person, not per deal; no per-deal reporting

defects.title: Location workflow clones
defects.impact: Every change = 3+ edits; per-location SMS hardcoded

defects.title: Click IDs never captured
defects.evidence: 0/11,077 contacts have gclid or fbclid populated
defects.impact: Ad attribution broken at collection; WF 13 has no payload to send

defects.title: TCPA consent never stored
defects.evidence: 0/11,077 contacts on any of 5 consent fields
defects.impact: No stored opt-in proof; backfill cannot enroll safely

defects.title: Hardcoded addresses in SMS
defects.evidence: WF 03 body contains '996 First Colonial Rd Virginia Beach'

defects.title: Reminder cadence ≠ documented plan
defects.impact: Show-rate measurement vs documented plan diverges

defects.title: Single-writer for outcomes is convention only
defects.evidence: GHL does not enforce sole writer; any source can overwrite
defects.impact: Sale Outcome can be silently re-corrupted post-fix

migration_steps.name: Revenue backfill - dry-run CSV
migration_steps.gate: Human review + dry-run CSV reconciles to opportunity values

migration_steps.name: Build 16 target WFs as drafts in prod
Dormant engine tag freeze + watch (prompt: s4-freeze)
Canary cutover: 48h cohort, then 7 clean business days parallel run
migration_steps.gate: 48h canary clean + 7 clean business days + approval

migration_steps.name: Cleanup after 7 clean business days (incl. explicit retirements)
migration_steps.gate: 7 clean business days since cutover; no alerts on weekly QA

decisions.choice: Merge into WF-03 Booking Confirmation and Reminders
R1 reminder cadence (T-3d / T-1d / morning-of / T-2h)

Rejected - no custom objects. Attribution stays as Contact fields + source_* tags; consent unifies to the canonical sms_consent_status Contact field (the 9 legacy consent fields reconcile into it and the orphan consent_log custom object is deleted in Step 6); revenue rolls up on the Opportunity.

decisions.decision: New vs Renewal is a first-class dimension
decisions.choice: Every opportunity is classified Sale Type = New | Renewal at PCC disposition (opportunity.sale_type). Drives pipeline routing, message variants, KPI segmentation, and referral-reward eligibility (new only).

Assume consent for the 11,077 historical contacts (ACCEPTED RISK). Rationale: existing member and lead relationships; native DND/STOP and 8 AM to 9 PM Contact-Timezone quiet hours go live first as guardrails; every SMS workflow filters on the canonical sms_consent_status field. Owner accepts residual TCPA exposure on legacy records.

'06. Price Calculator', standalone 'Intake Reminder', the Vercel 'Outbound Webhook', and 'New Workflow : 1780243709697' (unnamed, published; confirm before retiring) are deliberately retired at cutover (dropped, not silently orphaned). WF-04 assumes all intake-chasing responsibility. Retirement is executed in Step 6 (p5-cleanup) after confirming no live dependency.

Cutover gate is a 48-hour canary cohort followed by 7 clean business days of parallel run (clean = error rate < 0.1%, won-with-$0 = 0, no quiet-hours violations, no non-WF-05 outcome writes). The plan deliberately redesigns while migrating (Decision 6) rather than rebuild-then-optimize, justified by the severity of the auto-Won defect (D1, 939 false wins); the extended parallel run offsets the added risk.

Canonical WF-01..WF-16 = the workflow list + master-diagram scheme. All diagrams are renumbered to match; a crosswalk (old number to canonical) is shown on /to-be. 'Retention and Renewal' is a labeled Renewal Reminders sub-flow of WF-09 (Long-Term Nurture), not a separate top-level workflow.

risks.area: Revenue truth must be unambiguous inside GHL
risks.mitigation: Opportunity monetary_value (= net collected, netTotal after referral discount) is the single revenue truth; total_program_amount is gross. Evidence-split the 939 historical A&D wins into 3 buckets (clear-Won / clear-Lost / ambiguous -> human review).

Assume-consent is an accepted risk for the 11,077 historical contacts (Decision 8). Controls live first: native DND/STOP, 8 AM to 9 PM Contact-Timezone quiet hours, and every SMS workflow filtering on canonical sms_consent_status. Backfill itself stays silent: pause workflows, non-triggering API writes, assert sends=0.
Leadership sign-off before touching won/lost; grandfather historical commissions on the 939 reclassified wins.

Re-gate or scrub public site; PHI out of repo; encrypt change CSV; BAA on EMR to GHL sync. SMS bodies carry appointment/consultation logistics only - no symptom or treatment specifics (SMS bodies are scrubbed).
Ambiguous never auto-enroll; suppress sends until human-verified
Delta-aware writes: skip records changed since snapshot, or hard freeze window for write pass
Spike-test triggers/merge/reporting; denormalize click-ID + source on Opp as fallback
Drain or migrate in-flight enrollments; assert no contact in both old + new workflow

Only the PCC Sales Form writes outcomes (WF-05 routes, never writes). Add appointment-status 'Modified By' detection + monitor/alert on Sale Outcome changes not from the form; weekly data-quality report.

risks.area: Location field drives routing while being re-homed
risks.mitigation: Validate every location value pre-cutover; default branch + alert on unknown/blank
Continuous watch for engine activity; written confirmation engine stays off
Coordinate with media buyer; expect learning reset; verify click ID still readable
Order phases by irreversibility: pause → field writes → deletes & messaging last
Gate on automated invariants + stratified sampling, not raw CSV review
Scope manifest + risk register are the control; ship value-ordered (money-bleed first)

risks.area: Consult type (New vs Renewal) not captured or misclassified on the opportunity
risks.mitigation: Require opportunity.sale_type at Win (default New); backfill-audit on migration; split KPIs new vs renewal so renewal revenue is never counted as new-patient acquisition (would break CAC/LTV) and renewals never trigger referral rewards.

guardrails: Never workflow-move opportunities (wipes custom fields) - all migration is API-side
Never delete a pipeline before it is empty (deletes its opportunities)
Never rename or delete engine tags (BotFanatics / Sniper surface stays frozen)
Never write without a dry-run that emits a reviewable diff
Never treat a nosale (Advised and Declined) as an ad conversion: nosale is not advertising, so exclude it from WF-13 CAPI
Never mark an opportunity Won without Sale Type (New/Renewal) set - it drives revenue split, pipeline routing, and commissions
Never treat a Renewal as new-patient acquisition - exclude renewals from referral rewards and keep new vs renewal revenue separate in reporting
Every SMS and voicemail workflow filters on the canonical sms_consent_status field AND native DND, and runs inside a Time Window of 8 AM to 9 PM in the Contact Timezone (TCPA quiet hours).

Only the PCC Sales Form writes opportunity.sale_outcome, sale_type, appt_status, patient_care_consultant_id, and value - WF-05 is a router that reacts to those values and never writes outcomes itself.
No SMS or voicemail contains symptom or treatment specifics: appointment and consultation logistics only.

messages_tobe.id_name: WF-09-E2: Long-Term Nurture, Sleep and Performance Email
messages_tobe.message:
Hi {{first_name}},

Sleep is where recovery happens. It is when the body repairs tissue, consolidates memory, regulates mood, and recalibrates hormone levels. When sleep quality declines, everything downstream is affected: energy, focus, physical performance, body composition, and drive.

Most men over 35 attribute poor sleep to stress or screen time. Both play a role. But declining testosterone and imbalanced cortisol patterns are frequently overlooked contributors.

**The cycle looks like this:**

1. Hormonal decline disrupts sleep architecture, reducing time in deep and REM sleep.
2. Poor sleep quality further suppresses testosterone production.
3. Lower testosterone increases cortisol sensitivity, making stress feel more acute.
4. Elevated cortisol further disrupts sleep.

It is a feedback loop, and addressing one variable without examining the others yields limited results.

A comprehensive lab panel measures testosterone, cortisol, thyroid function, and metabolic markers. Our team can identify where the cycle is breaking down and build a plan to interrupt it.

For many men, this is not a lifestyle issue first. It is a medical issue that deserves proper assessment and physician-led care.

**CTA:** [SCHEDULE THE APPOINTMENT](BookMWC.com)

messages_tobe.id_name: WF-09-E3: Long-Term Nurture, Medical Authority Email
messages_tobe.message:
Hi {{first_name}},

The men's health space has expanded significantly in recent years. Telehealth platforms, subscription services, and franchise centers all offer some version of hormone optimization. The language is often similar. The difference is in the model of care.

**What physician-led care looks like at Men's Wellness:**

- **In-person lab work.** Labs are drawn on-site at the center, not ordered through a third-party lab. Results in approximately 15 minutes.
- **Face-to-face physician review.** Our team reviews results and explains what your numbers mean in the context of symptoms and goals. This is not a video call or an asynchronous message.
- **Individualized plan design.** Your optimization plan is built from labs. Not a template. Not a standard protocol. Our team designs it for your biology.
- **Ongoing monitoring.** Regular lab draws track your body's response. Our team adjusts dosages and approach based on data, not on a fixed schedule.

Men's Wellness is independent practice with over a decade of clinical experience in Virginia, a 4.9-star rating across 200+ reviews, and more than 10,000 members served. LegitScript certified and HIPAA compliant.

If options are being weighed, one in-person conversation with our team can quickly clarify whether optimization is appropriate, what it would look like, and what it would cost.

**CTA:** [BOOK THE APPOINTMENT](BookMWC.com)

{{pcc_name}}
Men's Wellness

[Standard Privacy Footer]

messages_tobe.id_name: WF-09-E4: Long-Term Nurture, Re-Engage Breakup Email
messages_tobe.message:
Hi {{first_name}},

This is the last scheduled message. Information about how men's health is approached at Men's Wellness has been shared, and time is respected.

If the symptoms that originally prompted your inquiry, whether low energy, difficulty with focus, poor sleep, or changes in drive, are still present, they are worth addressing. These are not conditions that improve with time alone. They are medical signals from a hormonal system that responds to physician-led care.

Whenever the time is right, the complimentary appointment is still available. On-site labs with 15-minute results, a one-on-one physician review, and a plan built from data. No obligation. Clear pricing before any decision is made.

**CTA:** [SCHEDULE WHEN READY](BookMWC.com)

Automated messages will cease after this email. To reconnect, reply to this email, text, or call. The team is here.

{{pcc_name}}
Men's Wellness

[Standard Privacy Footer]

messages_tobe.id_name: WF-06-E4: Review and Referral, Google Review Email
messages_tobe.message:
Hi {{first_name}},

Men's Wellness has maintained a 4.9-star rating across more than 200 Google reviews. Each of those reviews was left by a member who chose to share their experience.

A brief Google review makes a meaningful difference. It helps other men in Virginia who are searching for physician-led care find a practice they can trust.

The review does not need to be lengthy. A few sentences about the experience, the results, or what the process was like is valuable.

**CTA:** [LEAVE A REVIEW]({{review_link}})

Thank you for being part of the Men's Wellness community.

Your confidence.

Low testosterone doesn’t fix itself.

You were seconds away from booking your appointment.

messages_asis.subject: This is not a pill mill or a quick fix
messages_asis.message:
96

-

Let’s be honest {{contact.first_name}}
There are plenty of places that promise quick fixes. That’s not what we do.
